sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Stephanie Morales, is a 36-year-old female presenting with complaints of ankle pain and a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She reports that her ankle pain started 2 days ago after slipping on ice and twisting her left ankle.
She describes the pain as being on the outside of her ankle, with some swelling and tenderness to palpation.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with some minor relief.
She denies any bleeding or numbness in her ankle.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes, for which she is taking metformin.
Her blood sugars have been stable, with an HbA1c of 6.8.
She also has a history of high blood pressure, for which she is taking norvasc.
She has not seen the ophthalmologist recently for her diabetes checkup, but has an upcoming appointment in the next couple of weeks.
Review of systems: The patient denies any pain or symptoms in other organs, such as the calf, lower leg, or knee.
Current medications:
Metformin [dosage not mentioned] [frequency not mentioned] for diabetes
Norvasc [dosage not mentioned] [frequency not mentioned] for high blood pressure
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is normal, with no bruits.
Her lungs are clear, and her heart exam is normal, except for a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that has not changed from previous exams.
Her belly exam is normal.
On her ankle exam, she has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the 5th metatarsal, with some swelling and redness.
She has normal sensation and pulses in her ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, but further evaluation with an X-ray is needed to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; diabetes; high blood pressure; possible fracture (suspected).
Plan:
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I ordered an X-ray of the ankle to rule out a fracture.
I put the patient in a splint and gave her crutches to avoid weightbearing.
I will give her a follow-up call if the X-ray shows a fracture and send her to orthopedics if necessary.
I will also order baseline labs to check her blood sugars and kidney function.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in a couple of weeks to check on the results of the X-ray and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
She will also continue to follow up with her eye appointment for her diabetes checkup and continue with her metformin and norvasc as prescribed.
